passages.passage_text:
In the following questions, the symbols ©, ©, @, @ are used as follows.
A © B means A > B
A © B means A ≥ B
A = B means A = B
A © B means A ≤ B
A © B means A < B
Now, in each of the following questions, assuming the given statements to be true, find which of the two Conclusions I and II given below them is/are definitely true.
Give answer
(a) if only Conclusion I is true
(b) if only Conclusion II is true
(c) if either Conclusion I or II is true
(d) if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
(e) if both Conclusions I and II are true

In the following questions, the symbol @, @, =, © and © are used with following meanings.
P @ Q ⇒ P is greater than Q.
P © Q ⇒ P is smaller than Q.
P = Q ⇒ P is equal to Q.
P @ Q ⇒ P is either greater than or equal to Q.
P © Q ⇒ P is either smaller than or equal to Q.
Give answer
(a) if only Conclusion I is true
(b) if only Conclusion II is true
(c) if either Conclusion I or II is true
(d) if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
(e) if both Conclusions I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements H % J, J © N, N @ R
Conclusions I. R % J    II. H @ J    III. N @ H

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements M @ J, J $ T, T © N
Conclusions I. N # J    II. T % M    III. M @ N

Conclusions I. P @ D    II. K # P    III. F $ D

questions.body:

Conclusions I. R # M    II. N # D    III. N $ R

questions.body:

Conclusions I. K @ R    II. R % P    III. Q % K

questions.body:

Conclusions I. B @ Z    II. B = Z

choices.e: if both Conclusions I and II are true
